questions.question:
Each of these items has question followed by two statements. As the answer, mark

a. if the questions can be answered with the help of both the statements but not with the help of either statement itself.

b. if the question can not be answered even with the help of both the statements.

c. if the question can be answered with the help of statement \( II \) alone

d. if the question can be answered with the help of statement \( I \) alone

What is the number \( x \) if

I. The LCM of x and 18 is 36.

II. The HCF of x and 18 is 2.



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1995 | Question: 92
options: If the questions can be answered with the help of both the statements but not with the help of either statement itself.
If the question can not be answered even with the help of both the statements.
If the question can be answered with the help 0 statement \( II \) alone
If the question can be answered with the help of statement \( I \) alone

questions.question:
Each of these items has question followed by two statements. As the answer, mark

a. if the questions can be answered with the help of both the statements but not with the help of either statement itself.

b. if the question can not be answered even with the help of both the statements.

d. if the question can be answered with the help of statement \( I \) alone

If x, y and z are real numbers, is z - x even or odd?

I. xyz is odd.

II. xy + yz + zx is even.


Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1995 | Question: 93

Each of these items has question followed by two statements. As the answer, mark

What is value of x, if x and y are consecutive positive even integers?

a. if the questions can be answered with the help of both the statements but not with the help of either statement itself.

b. if the question can not be answered even with the help of both the statements.

c. if the question can be answered with the help 0 statement II alone

d. if the question can be answered with the help of statement I alone

I. \( (x - y)^{2} = 4 \).

II. \( (x + y)^{2} < 100 \)


Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1995 | Question: 94

What is the profit percent?

I. The cost price is \( 80 \)% of the selling price.

II. The profit is Rs. \( 50 \).



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1995 | Question: 95
options: if the questions can be answered with the help of both the statements but not with the help of either statement itself.
if the question can not be answered even with the help of both the statements.
if the question can be answered with the help 0 statement II alone
if the question can be answered with the help of statement I alone

questions.question:
What is the price of bananas?

I. With Rs. \( 84 \), I can buy \( 14 \) bananas and \( 35 \) oranges.

II. If price of bananas if reduced by \( 50 \)% then we can buy \( 48 \) bananas in Rs. \( 12 \).



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1995 | Question: 97
options: if the questions can be answered with the help of both the statements but not with the help of either statement itself.
if the question can not be answered even with the help of both the statements.
if the question can be answered with the help \( 0 \) statement \( II \) alone
if the question can be answered with the help of statement \( I \) alone

questions.question:
Is x + y – z + t even?

I. x + y + t is even.

II. tz is odd.



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1995 | Question: 99
options: if the questions can be answered with the help of both the statements but not with the help of either statement itself.
if the question can not be answered even with the help of both the statements.
if the question can be answered with the help 0 statement II alone
if the question can be answered with the help of statement I alone
